input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-15T01:06:07.711Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to supply values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates automatically — no event handling needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor` extension methods:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or setup method:
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
title.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For non-color properties, use the generic SetAppTheme<T>:
var icon = new Image();
icon.SetAppTheme<string>(Image.SourceProperty,
    light: "icon_light.png",
    dark:  "icon_dark.png");
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts live** | Bindings update instantly when the OS theme changes |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define `AppThemeBinding` values in `App.xaml` styles rather than inline |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme, or `AppTheme.Unspecified` to follow the system |
| **Detect current theme** | Read `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` at runtime |

For larger apps, move colors into an `App.xaml` `ResourceDictionary` with styles so you define each theme color once instead of repeating `AppThemeBinding` on every element.

This is a knowledge question — they want a code example and explanation, not changes to an existing codebase. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
